latest.title: Notice of GM
latest.content:
1 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6766C
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
01 May 2026
1 May 2026
PINEAPPLE POWER CORPORATION PLC
('the Company')
Notice of General Meeting
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC (LSE: PNPL), a special purpose acquisition company, is publishing a Circular to be sent to shareholders today, containing a Notice convening a General Meeting of the Company to be held at the offices of Fladgate LLP, 16 Great Queen Street, London WC2B 5DG at 11.00 a.m. on 21 May 2026. Copies of the Circular containing the Notice of General Meeting and a Form of Proxy have been posted to shareholders that have elected to receive documentation in hard copy format and are also available on the Company's website at https://www.pineapple-powercorp.com/news/, and a copy will be added to the National Storage Mechanism of the FCA at:-
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
At the General Meeting, the Company will propose a capital reorganisation that will consist of a sub-division by which each Existing Ordinary Share will be sub-divided into one New Ordinary Share of £0.001 each and one new Deferred Share of £0.009 each. The proposed Sub-Division necessitates an alteration to the Company's Articles to include the rights and restrictions attached to the Deferred Shares, and so changes to the Articles of the Company are proposed.
The timetable of events is:
Publication and posting of this Circular: 1 May 2026.
Latest time and date for lodging Forms of Proxy or for submitting proxy instructions via the CREST electronic proxy appointment service: 11.00 a.m. on 19 May 2026.
Record time for those Shareholders on the register of members entitled to attend or vote at the General Meeting: 11.00 a.m. 19 May 2026.
Time and date of the General Meeting: 11.00 a.m. on 21 May 2026.
Announcement of the result of the General Meeting: 21 May 2026.
Record Date: 6.00 p.m. on 21 May 2026.
Effective time of the Sub-Division, issue of New Ordinary Shares and admission of the New Ordinary Shares: 8.00 a.m. on 22 May 2026.
- END -

latest.title: Annual Financial Report Delay
latest.content:
Today 18:00
RNS Number : 7188B
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
23 April 2026
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
Annual Financial Report Delay
(the "Company")
April 23, 2026
Delay in Publication of the Annual Financial Report for the Year Ended 31 December 2025
Pineapple Power Corporation plc (LSE: PNPL)
, a special purpose acquisition company, announces a delay in the publication of its audited annual financial report for the year ended 31 December 2025 (the
"Annual Report"
), which was due to be released by 30 April 2026.
The delay has arisen as the Board and its advisors work to conclude certain matters required to stabilise and strengthen the Company's financial position. These discussions are ongoing and are a priority for the Board to ensure the "going concern" basis of the financial statements is robustly supported.
A further update regarding the expected publication date will be provided in due course.

latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
19 Feb 2026 18:15
RNS Number : 6748T
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
19 February 2026
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
Board Changes
(the "Company")
The Board of Pineapple Power Corporation PLC (LSE: PNPL) announces with great sadness that Andrew Holland, Non
‑
Executive Director of the Company, passed away on 16 January 2026. Mr Holland served on the Board since May 2017, contributing nearly nine years of dedicated and valued service. The Board extends its sincere condolences to Mr Holland's family and expresses its gratitude for his significant contribution to the Company throughout his tenure.
The Company announces that Mr Adam Colton Park has been appointed as an Independent Non
‑
Executive Director with effect from 10 February 2026. Mr Park brings extensive experience as a non
‑
executive director and chief operating officer across several organisations, with particular expertise in corporate governance, compliance, transaction management and strategic oversight.
Mr Park served as a Non Executive Director of AMTE Power plc having been appointed on 27 October 2016. The company entered administration on 19 January 2024.
Chairman, Claudio Morandi, commented:
"On behalf of the Board, I would like to express our profound sadness at the passing of Andrew. His guidance and support were greatly valued, and our thoughts are with his family at this difficult time.
We are also pleased to welcome Adam to the Board. His extensive experience as a COO and Non-Executive Director, combined with a strong track record in leading organisations through growth, strategic change and complex transitions, will bring significant insight to Pineapple Power. His pragmatic, governance-focused approach, along with his ability to work effectively with investors and leadership teams, makes him an excellent addition at this stage of the Company's development."
Save as disclosed above, there is no further information to be disclosed pursuant to UKLR 6.4.8R(2) to UKLR 6.4.8R(6).

latest.title: Restoration of Listing
latest.content:
4 Sep 2025 08:00
RNS Number : 9002X
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC
04 September 2025
This announcement contains inside information as stipulated under the UK version of the Market Abuse Regulation No 596/2014 which is part of English Law by virtue of the European (Withdrawal) Act 2018, as amended. On publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this information is considered to be in the public domain
September 4, 2025
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC ("Company" or "Pineapple")
Termination of proposed acquisition of Buffalo Battery Metals PTY Limited (Buffalo)Request to lift suspension of Listing and resumption of trading
Pineapple Power Corporation PLC previously announced on 2 May 2025 the proposed acquisition of Buffalo Battery Metals PTY. Despite significant effort by both parties, Pineapple and Buffalo have mutually agreed to discontinue discussions on the proposed acquisition.
Accordingly, Pineapple has applied to the FCA to lift the suspension of the Company's ordinary shares from the Official List and from trading on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange (LON:LSEG). The Company anticipates trading will resume shortly.
Pineapple considered the proposed acquisition internally and as such no external due diligence costs have been incurred in connection with the proposed acquisition.
Clive de Larrabeiti, Corporate Finance Advisor to the Company stated - "
It is regretful that the proposed amalgamation with Buffalo Battery Metals could not be completed despite the best efforts of both parties. However, the resumption of trading on the Main Market of the LSE will bring additional opportunities for the company and its shareholders as Pineapple is now one of very few available cash shells still listed on this market which enables an expeditious route to the international financial markets.
I am pleased to report that the Company has, over the past month, received a number of enquiries from interested companies, which we are actively exploring and will update the market in due course.
"
ENQUIRIES
For further information, please visit -
www.pineapple-powercorp.com

company_30s.what_they_do: Pineapple Power Corporation is a cash‑shell company that has been looking to merge with or take over another business through a reverse takeover. Its main goal is to become a listed entity that can then launch a new product line or service, but it has no operating revenue yet.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a niche where private tech firms look for a quick route to the public markets. If the reverse takeover succeeds, the combined entity could bring a new technology (e.g., medical AI from FUSE‑AI) to market, giving it a foothold in a fast‑growing industry.
company_30s.current_state: PNPL has been suspended for a period, then reinstated on the London market. It remains a shell with no revenue, high cash burn risk, and a history of repeated attempts at a reverse takeover. The share price is trading at a steep discount to its previous highs.

rns_narrative: The company’s recent filings show a change in directors and a pause on its annual report, indicating an attempt to regroup after a suspension. The 4‑September filing confirms the share was restored, while earlier announcements hinted at a potential reverse takeover of FUSE‑AI. The pattern suggests the firm is focusing on completing a deal that would bring real assets into the business.

Two main themes emerge: 1) Governance and listing stability – the company is clearing regulatory hurdles to keep its shares on the market. 2) Reverse takeover activity – it is actively seeking a target that can provide operational depth and a new revenue stream.
These moves signal that the company is trying to shift from a shell to a viable entity, which could act as a catalyst for a price rebound.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Hopeful yet skeptical – people are waiting for a deal but wary of repeated failures.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders see the RTO announcement as a sign that the company will finally have a product to sell, potentially lifting the share to pre‑suspension levels.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to the lack of a real business, the repeated delays in reporting, and the risk of another delisting if the takeover stalls.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat; there is little chatter after the restoration, but occasional bursts of hope appear around RTO news.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is dead – no recent search activity, which is a classic contrarian indicator.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the reverse takeover with FUSE‑AI, which would give the company a tangible product line.
A positive half‑year report that shows cash on hand and a clear path to revenue.
A surge in trading volume that exceeds the average by 5‑10 times, indicating renewed investor interest.
Any announcement that the company has secured additional funding to support the takeover.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike coupled with a clear statement from the board about the RTO deadline. A sudden jump in the share’s trading price above its 20‑day moving average would also signal a potential bounce.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early stages of a recovery cycle. The APEX score shows decent compression, meaning the price is in a tight range and could break out once a catalyst arrives. The deep drawdown suggests that if the takeover succeeds, the mean reversion could be sharp.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the reverse takeover goes through, the company will suddenly have a product to market and a new source of revenue. A 70‑95% crash means the current price is far below its pre‑suspension value. Recovering to just 50% of the pre‑crash price would double the current price, which is a huge upside for a small position.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has no operating history and has faced multiple listing suspensions. If the takeover fails or the company is forced to delist again, the stock could fall back to its current level or lower. The worst‑case is a complete wipe‑out of the investment if the shell is liquidated.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: up to 200% if the share recovers to halfway between its current price and the pre‑suspension level. Potential downside: limited to the current price because the stock is already at a deep trough. The risk/reward ratio is therefore highly asymmetric in favor of the trader.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the share closes above the 20‑day moving average with volume at least 5 times the 20‑day average. Allocate 5–10% of the trading capital to this position and set a stop 10–15% below the entry to protect against a brief pullback.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a clear announcement of the reverse takeover or a sustained volume spike. Watch the board meeting minutes and any press release that confirms the target company.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a second delisting or declares bankruptcy, exit immediately. Also exit if the reverse takeover falls through and the share remains stagnant for 90 days.

ai_insights.bottom_line: PNPL sits at a deep crash, and a successful reverse takeover could trigger a sharp rebound – a classic high‑asymmetry contrarian play.
